headline: Same Floods, Same Areas, Same Speeches, Every Year
context: The December 2025 floods displaced 120,000 people across Pahang, Kelantan, and Terengganu — the same districts as 2014, 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024.

cards.big: Six floods in six years. Same postcodes.
cards.sub: NADMA's post-flood reports recommend the same infrastructure every time.

cards.big: RM12B allocated for flood mitigation since 2015. 34% disbursed.
cards.sub: Auditor-General flagged incomplete drainage projects in four consecutive reports.

cards.big: Logging concessions upstream increased 22% in three affected states.
cards.sub: State land use decisions directly contradict federal flood mitigation plans.

cards.big: Federal-state jurisdiction split means nobody owns the problem.
cards.sub: Water is federal. Land is state. Drainage is local. Accountability is nowhere.

cards.big: Floods are not natural disasters when the causes are policy choices.
cards.sub: Upstream logging, blocked drainage, stalled mitigation — these are decisions.

cards.big: Until land use and flood mitigation sit under the same authority, the cycle will not break.
